content:
First, note that “\(n^2\) has the same remainder upon division by 7, 11, 13” means there is a single integer \(r\in\{0,1,\dots,6\}\) so that

\[
n^2\equiv r\pmod7,\quad
n^2\equiv r\pmod{11},\quad
n^2\equiv r\pmod{13}.
\]

By CRT this is equivalent to
\[
n^2\equiv r\pmod{1001},
\]
with \(0\le r\le6\).  For each \(r\), the number of solutions \(n\pmod{1001}\) to \(n^2\equiv r\) is
\[
\prod_{p\in\{7,11,13\}}\#\{x\pmod p:x^2\equiv r\pmod p\}.
\]
When \(r=0\), each prime gives exactly one root, so there is 1 solution mod 1001.  For \(1\le r\le6\), one checks that only \(r=1\) or \(4\) is simultaneously a quadratic residue mod 7, 11, 13, and each of these gives \(2\) roots mod 7, \(2\) mod 11, \(2\) mod 13, for a total of \(2^3=8\) solutions mod 1001.  Hence in one full block of length 1001 there are
\[
1\;(\text{for }r=0)+8\;(\text{for }r=1)+8\;(\text{for }r=4)
\;=\;17
\]
solutions.

We need all positive \(n<2018\).  Since \(2017=2\cdot1001+15\), there are two full blocks of size 1001 and a final partial block of length 15.  Each full block contributes 17 solutions, and a quick check of \(n=1,2,\dots,15\) shows only \(n=1\) and \(n=2\) work in the partial block.  Thus the total is
\[
2\cdot17 \;+\;2 \;=\;36\,.
\]

Answer: \(\boxed{36}.\)

n^2\equiv r\pmod{1001},

\prod_{p\in\{7,11,13\}}\#\{x\pmod p:x^2\equiv r\pmod p\}.
\]
When \(r=0\), each prime gives exactly one root, so there is 1 solution mod 1001. \[
